persona.name: The anti-crypto edition collector
persona.description: People who love the '#7 of 50' feeling — owning a numbered edition from an artist they follow, with clear provenance — and are tired of wallet onboarding, seed phrases, gas fees, and a speculation culture they never asked for.
persona.frustration: Every 'digital ownership' product drags you through wallets, seed phrases, gas fees, and a flip-for-profit culture. The category made collecting feel like trading.

problem: Numbered digital editions should be about provenance and pride of collection — not wallets, gas, or speculation. The existing category is hostile to collectors who just want to own something beautiful. Vitrine is collecting given back to collectors.
positioning: Collecting Without the Chain — ownership as joy and provenance, not finance.
hero_line: Own the edition. Skip the wallet.

catalog.domain: numbered synthetic digital collectible editions (fictional catalog)

commerce_behavior.cart: single-edition (one edition per purchase, each is unique by number)
commerce_behavior.checkout: guest checkout — no wallet, no account required
commerce_behavior.fulfillment: ownership_granted — edition added to 'Your Vitrine' shelf immediately
commerce_behavior.refund_window: 24 hours, before edition is claimed by another buyer

rights_provenance.note: Sandbox only — all editions are synthetic. No real digital ownership, no real blockchain, no real artist contracts.
rights_provenance.media: Placeholder/generated images only — no real artwork committed

chainless_boundary.note: This boundary is a brand commitment, not just a technical limit. No wallet, chain, mint, token, gas, or blockchain surface — ever — in code, UI, or API calls. Brand copy negating these (e.g., 'no wallet', 'skip the wallet') is intentional and listed as known_false_positives in the chainless contract.

architecture.data_layer: packages/commerce-core (local deterministic engine; ownership_granted state; no blockchain)
architecture.ui_source: docs/atm-415/ui/vitrine — port verbatim, no visual changes

kpi_assumptions.nonclaim: Synthetic cohort only. Numbers do not predict or reflect real collector behavior. All iteration verdicts must carry the explicit synthetic-only nonclaim.

risks.description: UI port may deviate from provided design
risks.mitigation: impeccable critique gate >= 7/10, zero P0 findings, before qa passes

risks.description: The 'chainless' positioning is anti-crypto — scanner must not flag brand nonclaim copy
risks.mitigation: known_false_positives allowance in nft-chainless.json covers negation/disclaimer copy

risks.description: Edition ownership is synthetic — no real asset transfer
risks.mitigation: Footer + first-purchase nonclaim required; every verdict carries explicit synthetic nonclaim

nonclaims: does not prove real digital ownership or asset transfer
does not involve any blockchain, wallet, or token — ever
does not prove real artist licensing or rights
synthetic collectibles only — no financial value, no resale
synthetic cohort only — not real collector behavior
every iteration verdict must carry the explicit synthetic-only nonclaim

stop_conditions.ENGINEERING_REQUIRED: If the ownership_granted fulfillment state cannot be supported in commerce-core — stop and surface
stop_conditions.OWNER_GATE: Any change to frozen contracts/seeds/KPI after baseline; any real payment, blockchain, or external service integration

bounded_adaptation_candidate.description: Provenance prominence: '#7 of 50' and provenance block shown above the fold vs below — does edition-scarcity framing at the top move conversion?
